blogs.contentHtml:
## Introduction
Traveling to Japan is fascinating. It's a country with vibrant culture, rich history, and breathtaking landscapes. But navigating Japan's public transportation can be overwhelming, especially for tourists. That's where the Amaze card comes in - a convenient, cashless solution for travelers. The primary keyword, **Amaze card**, offers a seamless experience. We'll delve into its benefits and how it works with Suica, a popular prepaid card in Japan.

## Key Benefits
The Amaze card offers many benefits. It's convenient, allowing you to tap and go. You can say goodbye to carrying cash or waiting in line. 
It offers cashless payments, making it easy to pay for transportation and food. 
The Amaze card is widely accepted across Japan, including Tokyo, Osaka, and Kyoto.

## How it Works
So, how does the Amaze card work with Suica? It's straightforward. 
You add the Amaze card to your digital wallet. 
Then, you top up your Amaze card using your YouTrip card. 
Now, you can use the Amaze card for Suica, making travel easy.

## Tips for Using Amaze Card
To get the most out of your Amaze card, use it for all transactions. 
Keep your Amaze card topped up to avoid interruptions. 
Take advantage of cashback offers when topping up your Amaze card. 
Consider using the Amaze card with other services for a better travel experience.

## Conclusion
In conclusion, the Amaze card is convenient and cashless. 
It offers a seamless travel experience with Suica. 
With wide acceptance and easy top-up, the Amaze card is attractive to travelers. 
Sign up for the Amaze card today and discover a new way to travel in Japan.

sourceDocuments.extractedText: Using the Instarem amaze card in South Korea: T-money, fees and InstaPoints Skip to the content Home Blog Using the Instarem amaze card in South Korea: T-money, fees and InstaPoints Using the Instarem amaze card in South Korea: T-money, fees and InstaPoints Insider 20 Apr 2026 5 mins read Written by Team Instarem This article covers: What the amaze card gives you during overseas travel Why the amaze card works well for South Korea travel How InstaPoints work on T-money top-ups T-money top-up methods: Fees and compatibility compared Minimum top-up amount to earn InstaPoints How amaze reduces the cost of topping up in Korea Before you travel: amaze wallet checklist Your trip to South Korea deserves better than overpriced FX fees and clunky travel payments. The Instarem amaze card makes it easy to pay overseas with stellar exchange rates while earning rewards on every spend. Link up to 5 Singapore-issued Mastercard credit or debit cards, or use the amaze wallet to earn InstaPoints – easily convertible into KrisFlyer miles. From cafe hopping in Seoul to shopping in Myeongdong, get more for every won with amaze. If you’re using your card for T-money top-ups in Korea, there’s an extra reason to use amaze. You can earn InstaPoints on eligible wallet-funded transactions, as long as you meet the minimum spend requirement. Current guidance says amaze wallet transactions earn 0.5 InstaPoints per S$1 spent, with a minimum spend of S$10 per transaction to qualify. What the amaze card gives you during overseas travel The amaze card is built for overseas spending. It offers competitive FX conversion and helps users avoid the typical foreign transaction fees charged by banks, while also giving the flexibility to consolidate up to five Mastercard cards into one. That makes it especially useful for travel, where you want convenience, no hidden charges, and a smoother payment experience across transport, dining, shopping, and attractions. amaze also has no annual fee, which makes it easy to keep as your go-to travel card. Why the amaze card works well for South Korea travel South Korea is a very card-friendly destination, but transport is still easiest with a T-money card. T-money is widely used on subways, buses, and even at some convenience stores, and top-ups are commonly done in Korean won. Using amaze alongside your Korea travel spend can be a smart choice, especially if you are funding spend through the amaze wallet, because eligible wallet-funded transactions can earn InstaPoints. How InstaPoints work on T-money top-ups One of the useful things to know is that amaze now rewards InstaPoints only for wallet-funded transactions, not for standard linked-card transactions. From 10 March 2025, wallet-funded amaze spend earns 0.5 InstaPoints per S$1, and each transaction must be at least S$10 to qualify for points. For T-money top-ups, that means small reloads may not earn anything. If your transaction is below the minimum qualifying amount, you will not receive InstaPoints for that spend. T-money top-up methods: Fees and compatibility compared Feature Apple Wallet T-money Physical T-money Top-up Method In-app via amaze card Cash at stations or stores FX Rate Zero FX via amaze Zero FX via xfamaze at ATMs Currency Billed USD KRW (cash) Convenience High, fully digital Low, requires physical cash Minimum top-up amount to earn InstaPoints Top up above 12,000 KRW to improve your chances of crossing the S$10 minimum spend threshold needed to earn InstaPoints on eligible amaze wallet transactions. The exact SGD equivalent can vary with exchange rates, so keeping your top-up above 12,000 KRW gives you a safer buffer. If you want to be extra safe, you can round up slightly more, especially when exchange rates move. The main rule is simple, top up enough so the transaction clears the S$10 minimum. How amaze reduces the cost of topping up in Korea amaze stands out because it combines travel-friendly FX rates, rewards through InstaPoints on wallet spend, and the convenience of linking multiple Mastercard cards in one place. It is particularly appealing for Singapore-based travellers who want flexibility without carrying several cards. Choosing the best travel card depends on your spending habits, but amaze is clearly positioned as a strong option because of its low-fee overseas use, unlimited wallet rewards, and card-linking feature. Before you travel: amaze wallet checklist Before using amaze for T-money top-ups, make sure your spend is coming from the amaze wallet if your goal is to earn InstaPoints, because linked-card transactions no longer qualify for InstaPoints. And if you are topping up T-money in Korea, keep each top-up above 12,000 KRW so you are more likely to meet the minimum qualifying amount for rewards. What is a Suica Card in Japan? Image Credits: Gizmodo The Suica card is a rechargeable contactless smart card used for public transportation and cashless payments across Japan. It’s issued by JR East (East Japan Railway Company) and accepted on trains, buses, vending machines, convenience stores, and even some restaurants. With physical Suica cards being harder to find due to recent shortages, the virtual Suica card is now the easiest and most convenient way to get around — especially for Apple users. Suica Card Japan Guide: Heading to Japan? Check out The Ultimate Universal Studios Japan (USJ) Guide 2025 How to get a Virtual Suica Card on Apple Wallet Apple users, rejoice. You can set up a virtual Suica card on your iPhone in minutes and top it up using your YouTrip card for competitive JPY exchange rates. Here’s a step-by-step guide on how to get started: 1️⃣ Open your Wallet app > Tap the ‘+’ icon on the top right corner 2️⃣ Select ‘Travel Card’ > Key in ‘Suica’ 3️⃣ Tap ‘Continue’ > key in your desired JPY amount 4️⃣ Scan through the T&Cs & select ‘Agree’ 5️⃣ Double-click the side button to top up your Suica card 6️⃣ That’s it — your virtual Suica is ready to use in Japan! Besides Suica, you can also choose to add the PASMO and ICOCA virtual cards to your Apple Wallet. Do note that you can only top up your Suica card with a Mastercard!
